title: Accounting of Disclosures — Registry vs EHR-Logged Disclosures

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.528 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.528 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.41: Accounting of Disclosures — Registry vs EHR-Logged Disclosures.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.41' running on sourcetype hipaa:disclosure_log and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk DB Connect (2686)
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:disclosure_log" earliest=-30d
| stats count by disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class
| join type=left PAT_ID [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:disclosure_export" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(_time) as epic_export by PAT_ID ]
| where isnull(epic_export) AND recipient_class IN ("LawEnforcement","Insurance","Employer")
| table disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class, count
description: Reconciles disclosure events recorded in the accounting of disclosures repository with interface and manual disclosure logs so patients receive accurate histories.
value: Reconciles disclosure events recorded in the accounting of disclosures repository with interface and manual disclosure logs so patients receive accurate histories.
implementation: (1) Define authoritative system of record; (2) fix integration gaps when join misses; (3) legal review before patient-facing export.
visualization: Table (missing reconciliations), Column chart (disclosures by class).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Reconciles disclosure events recorded in the accounting of disclosures repository with interface and manual disclosure logs so patients receive accurate histories — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:disclosure_log` (`disclosure_id`, `PAT_ID`, `recipient_class`), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:disclosure_export`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define authoritative system of record; (2) fix integration gaps when join misses; (3) legal review before patient-facing export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:disclosure_log" earliest=-30d
| stats count by disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class
| join type=left PAT_ID [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:disclosure_export" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(_time) as epic_export by PAT_ID ]
| where isnull(epic_export) AND recipient_class IN ("LawEnforcement","Insurance","Employer")
| table disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Accounting of Disclosures — Registry vs EHR-Logged Disclosures (§164.528)** — Reconciles disclosure events recorded in the accounting of disclosures repository with interface and manual disclosure logs so patients receive accurate histories.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:disclosure_log` (`disclosure_id`, `PAT_ID`, `recipient_class`), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:disclosure_export`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:disclosure_log. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:disclosure_log", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(epic_export) AND recipient_class IN ("LawEnforcement","Insurance","Employer")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Accounting of Disclosures — Registry vs EHR-Logged Disclosures (§164.528)**): table disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class, count


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing reconciliations), Column chart (disclosures by class).
